meta_title: For Founders: debugging with AI to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Debugging with AI to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn to use AI-driven debugging to reverse-engineer profitable SaaS products using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'the next big idea' and start fixing broken workflows. By using AI to debug and refine existing, inefficient software processes, you identify massive market gaps. Use Antigravity to map system failures and Claude Code to prototype solutions, turning technical friction into a high-margin, scalable SaaS product.
content.intro:
The screen glows with a blinking cursor, mocking the emptiness of your project folder. You’ve spent weeks hunting for a 'unique' idea, only to find yourself paralyzed by the perfectionism of the blank page. The graveyard of abandoned startups is filled with founders who waited for inspiration instead of hunting for friction.

Real wealth isn't found in a visionary spark; it's found in the cracks of existing, broken systems. When you treat software as a series of debuggable problems, you stop being a dreamer and start being a solution architect. Using AI to systematically analyze and repair technical bottlenecks, you can reverse-engineer a product that customers are already desperate to buy.

steps.title: Identify Friction Points
steps.description: Scan open-source repositories or legacy SaaS forums for high-volume 'issue' tags. You aren't looking for code; you are looking for repeated human frustration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the top 50 GitHub issues in the [Target Niche] repo. Identify the recurring technical bottleneck causing the most developer friction and suggest a SaaS-based automation to resolve it.
steps.claude_code_command: claude search --query 'pain points in [Target Niche] workflows'

steps.title: Reverse-Engineer the Workflow
steps.description: Define the input-output map of the problem. What is the current manual process, and where does it fail?
steps.antigravity_prompt: Map the current manual workflow for [Process]. Identify the specific failure point where users abandon the process. Propose a lean architecture to automate this step.

steps.title: Prototyping the Fix
steps.description: Build the core engine of your solution. Focus strictly on the 'Heal' function—the logic that fixes the user's problem.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust, production-ready function to handle [Problem]. Ensure it includes error handling for edge cases commonly reported in [Target Niche].

steps.title: Stress-Testing with AI
steps.description: Use AI to act as a malicious user and a broken system to see if your solution holds up under pressure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a security auditor. Attempt to break the logic of this script using common edge cases and invalid inputs. Provide a patch for every vulnerability found.

steps.title: Packaging for SaaS
steps.description: Wrap your debugged utility into an API-first service that can be plugged into existing enterprise stacks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this logic into a scalable API endpoint. Include documentation for a RESTful interface and suggest a subscription-based rate limiting strategy.

pro_tips: Always look for 'deprecated' libraries in high-traffic niches; they represent a massive opportunity for a modern, AI-enhanced replacement.
Never build a full UI until your core debug logic has at least 10 beta users willing to pay for the fix.
Use Claude Code to generate your unit tests before you write the actual business logic to ensure TDD compliance.
Focus your 'debug' efforts on high-churn industries like FinTech or HR-Tech, where even small performance improvements command premium pricing.
Keep your MVP 'ugly' but your error handling 'beautiful'—founders sell reliability, not aesthetics.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment, reach out to the users you identified in Step 1, and offer them the fix in exchange for a pre-order or feedback session.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for the market. You have the tools, the logic, and the methodology to turn technical frustration into recurring revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
